race.name: Gran Fondo Asheville
race.tagline: Serpentine roads, climbs, and thrilling Blue Ridge Parkway descents through the mountains near Asheville, NC

vitals.location: Asheville Yards Amphitheater, 75 Coxe Ave, Asheville, NC 28801
vitals.date_specific: July 19, 2026 (packet pickup); main event July 20, 2026 (subject to confirmation)
vitals.registration: Open; details via official app and website
vitals.aid_stations: 3 (Gran), 2 (Medio), 1 (Piccolo); stocked with water, sports drinks, gels, bars, fruit, snacks

climate.overview: Mid-to-late July in Asheville: warm/summery (highs ~80-85°F/27-29°C), possible afternoon thunderstorms in mountains

terrain.overview: Winding roads through pastoral countryside to mountain ridge vistas; Blue Ridge Parkway descents; well-marked with timed segments

logistics.explanation: Central downtown start/finish, packet pickup on-site, Ride with GPS app for navigation/results, clear staging rules[1][2]

length.explanation: Classic gran fondo distances: 100/60/30 miles across three options[1][2][5][8]

technicality.explanation: Serpentine roads, climbs, descents; timed segments require precise riding on right side of road[1][2][4]

elevation.explanation: Significant mountain climbs in Blue Ridge area described, but exact figures unavailable[1][4][5]

climate.explanation: Summer heat with thunderstorm risk; no event-specific weather data[1][2]

altitude.explanation: Blue Ridge Mountains elevation, but no precise altitude data sourced

adventure.explanation: Mountain ridge vistas, pastoral countryside, thrilling Parkway descents from downtown Asheville[1][2][5]

prestige.explanation: Gran Fondo National Series franchise event with national championships history[2][4][5][6]

race_quality.explanation: Timed segments, real-time results, age-group podiums, custom winner kits, team competition[1][2][5]

experience.explanation: App-powered navigation, expo, awards ceremony, nonprofit support[1][2][3]

community.explanation: Benefits Asheville on Bikes nonprofit; post-disaster economic stimulus[2][3]

field_depth.explanation: No participant numbers available; national series qualifiers[1][5]

value.explanation: Multiple distances, aid stations, awards; registration cost unknown[1][2]

expenses.explanation: No fee data; central location may reduce travel costs

cultural_impact.explanation: Local economic boost and nonprofit support in rebuilding community[2]

biased_opinion.overview: Classic National Series gran fondo with mountainous adventure, timed competition, and community focus. Strong format but lacks precise elevation/field data.

logistics.overview: Start/finish at Asheville Yards Amphitheater. Packet pickup Sat 12-5pm, Sun 5:45am+. Staging by 7:45am, 8:00am start. Ride with GPS app required. Bib, bike plate, ankle timing chip mandatory. Right-side road for timed segments[1][2]

history.overview: Part of Gran Fondo National Series (America's original series). Hosted 2022 USA Cycling Gran Fondo National Championships. 2025 presented by Applewood Manor Foundation amid Asheville rebuild. 2026: July 19/20[2][4][5]

course_description.character: Downtown Asheville start → serpentine roads through countryside → mountain ridge climbs → Blue Ridge Parkway descent back to town. Timed segments (#2 Gran-only, #3/#4 multi-route). **Suffering zones:** mountain climbs, timed KOM segments[1][2][4][5]

non_negotiables: Right side of road for all timed segment start/finish lines
Timing chip on ankle; cross official start line
Bib on jersey back center, bike plate on handlebar/head tube
Ride with GPS app for routes/results
